tagline: "Every painting begins as an act of noticing — and ends as an invitation to do the same."

hero.body: Original works by Anisha Goyal — painted after the workday ends, with the patience of someone who has learned to look slowly.

about.name: Anisha Goyal
paragraphs: By day, I work as a business analyst — finding meaning in numbers, translating complexity into clarity. It is precise, logical, and satisfying. But it is not where I find beauty.

Painting is where I find beauty. My landscapes and still lifes begin with long, unhurried looking — at the way evening light lands on a hillside, or the quiet presence of fruit on a table. Each canvas is a record of that attention.
I work primarily in oil on canvas and linen. My subjects are landscapes and still lifes — ordinary moments made worth pausing for.

contact.heading: Happy to hear from you
contact.intro: For inquiries about available works, commissions, or anything else — write to me directly. I respond personally.

paintings.title: Morning Mist
paintings.desc: A study in atmospheric light — misty hills receding into a cool morning sky. Painted from memory after an early walk, before the world woke up.

paintings.title: Coastal Morning
paintings.desc: The coastline at first light, dunes and tufted grasses catching the low horizontal sun. The largest work in the current collection, painted over three weeks.

paintings.title: Mediterranean Still Life
paintings.desc: A ceramic bowl of lemons on a draped cloth. Painted slowly over several evenings, returning each time to the same arrangement and the same quiet light.

paintings.title: Golden Fields
paintings.desc: Late summer, the fields past their peak. A warm, unhurried palette that captures the particular quality of August — the heaviness of it, the gold.

paintings.title: Summer Bouquet
paintings.desc: Garden flowers in a simple white vase. A direct, joyful painting — made quickly and kept exactly as it came, without correction.

paintings.title: Into the Forest
paintings.desc: The interior of a mixed woodland at dusk. Dappled light falling through the canopy onto a path that leads somewhere you cannot quite see.

paintings.title: Autumn Harvest
paintings.desc: A field study from early autumn — warm ochres and ambers, the stubble of a recently harvested crop, the long shadows of late afternoon.

paintings.title: The Lemon Bowl
paintings.desc: A small, intimate still life. Three lemons in a terracotta bowl, painted in a single sitting on a rainy Sunday. One of my favourites.

paintings.title: Woodland Path
paintings.desc: An evening walk into the trees. The path disappearing into green shadow, the last light of the day filtering through from somewhere behind you.
